questions.question: Describe the main difference between a flat distributed transaction and a nested distributed transaction. Provide an example to illustrate your answer.
What is the purpose of a two-phase commit protocol in distributed transactions? Explain its steps and how it ensures consistency across a distributed system.

A bank system uses a flat distributed transaction to transfer funds between two accounts. If the transfer fails, the system rolls back all changes made during the transaction. What are the advantages and disadvantages of using a flat distributed transaction in this scenario?
